narrative.headline: What 2018 Mazda Mazda3 owners report most
narrative.summary: Owners filed 107 complaints to NHTSA about the 2018 Mazda Mazda3. Electrical system problems lead the list at 30 reports (28%), followed by wiper/visibility and back-over prevention issues. Most reports describe no crash or injury, but a handful do: the wiper and engine categories each note injuries, and one engine complaint involved a fatal crash. Read the details below before drawing conclusions.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most-reported area, with 30 complaints (28% of all reports). One of these involved a crash; none reported a fire or injury.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: 16 complaints (15% of reports) about visibility and the wipers. One involved a crash and 3 people were injured across these reports.

issues.sample: The contact owns a 2018 Mazda Mazda3. The contact received notification of NHTSA Campaign Number: 23V487000 (Back Over Prevention) however, the part to do the recall repair was unavailable. The local dealer was contacted, and it was confirmed that the part was not yet available.
issues.title: Back Over Prevention
issues.blurb: 15 complaints (14% of reports) tied to back-over prevention, such as the rear camera. One involved a crash; none reported a fire or injury.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 12 complaints (11% of reports) about the engine. These are the most serious in the set: one involved a crash, 2 people were injured, and 1 death was reported.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 7 complaints (7% of reports) about the fuel and propulsion system. None reported a crash, fire, or injury.
